mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and investors, thereby supporting higher equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in the unemployment rate indicates a strengthening labor market, leading to increased consumer confidence and spending, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to show statistically significant positive 60-day returns (p-value > 0.1) following triggered UNRATE declines over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low interest rates and robust retail sales indicates strong consumer demand supported by affordable credit. This environment fosters increased corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: consumer-driven expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If the interaction term between low FEDFUNDS and positive RSAFS growth does not significantly explain S&P 500 20-day returns (t-stat < 2) in a regression model, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signifies robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating strong underlying economic health. This growth translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) in more than 40% of periods following a triggered INDPRO acceleration, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation signals potential overheating in the economy, which typically prompts the central bank to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the CPIAUCSL trigger condition is met, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistently high housing starts reflect strong consumer and business confidence, indicating robust demand in the construction sector and related industries. This broad-based economic activity supports overall market performance.
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST levels and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and rising retail sales signify a healthy and confident consumer base with both the income and willingness to spend. This directly boosts corporate revenues and overall market sentiment, leading to positive returns.
failure_condition: If periods of joint strong PAYEMS and RSAFS growth do not yield significantly higher S&P 500 60-day returns compared to other periods (t-test p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) signals a broad economic downturn or recession, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations, increased uncertainty, and a subsequent decline in S&P 500 equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below -0.5%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods following a triggered GDPC1 contraction, this hypothesis is rejected.
